user_prompt: I know you just deleted it, but we need a `WifiApModeView` that is a `FlyGuiView`. This will display Wi-Fi credentials while in soft AP mode.
Place `sprit_wifi_50` in the top left corner (but not covering the top bar). If this is the default super safe randomly generated soft AP, then also put `sprite_security_50` beside it.

The bottom right corner, also linked to `BtnC`, put in a clickable `FlyGuiItem` that switches between `sprit_eye_closed_100` and `sprit_eye_open_100` when touched. Open eye means the info on the screen is displayed, closed means the sensitive parts are displayed as "**********"
Below the Wi-Fi icon, there should be 5 lines of text, similar to below
IP: <IP of the internal web server, probably 192.168.4.1>
Below the IP line, use the smallest font, show a line of text that cycles through a few displayed data items, cycling every 3 seconds, the items are:
* "page loads: n", number of times the "/get_info" request has been served since starting the AP
* "logins: n", number of times the "/get_cfg" request has been served since starting the AP, it is not displayed under security-level 0
* "saves: n", number of times the "/set_cfg" request (also the reset password and reset memory) has been served since starting the AP
* "errors: n", number of times there has been an apparent error, such as security-level mismatch error, or, some form of authentication error, those are the important ones
* "downloads: n", number of times a file has been downloaded, also track deletions and uploads with this number even though it doesn't make sense
`WifiManager` needs getters for these, and they need to be reset when the Wi-Fi starts, there may be request URL handlers missing but we'll implement those later

I am aware that I did not include some sort of stop button. The user must either click the physical reset button or power button to exit. So add a red line of text, small, as the next line, hinting so. I have no idea if it fits on the screen but do it so I can see.
agent_reply: - Shows `sprit_wifi_50` at the top-left below the top bar.
- Shows `sprit_security_50` beside it for the generated secure AP.
- Shows SSID/password/IP, with SSID/password hidden as `**********` by default.
- Adds the bottom-right eye toggle using `sprit_eye_closed_100` / `sprit_eye_open_100`, touchable and also tied to `BtnC`.
- Cycles the small stats line every 3 seconds.
- Adds the red â€śreset or power offâ€ť exit hint.
- Added â€śDefault Secure APâ€ť to the Wi-Fi scroll list.
- Selecting it starts `WifiManager::startGeneratedSoftAp()` and shows the AP view.
- Selecting a configured AP starts that AP and shows the same view.
- Added `WifiManager` web counter getters and reset-on-AP-start behavior.
- Counted `/get_info`, `/get_cfg`, `/set_cfg`, reset password/memory, downloads/deletes/uploads, and notable errors.

- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` and `pio run -e thefly_sec1` both compile the changed files successfully, then still fail at final link with the existing IRAM overflow: `iram0_0_seg` overflowed by `2152 bytes`.

agent_reply: `WifiApModeView` now draws two smallest-font lines at the top-right area starting at `x=104`:
- connected SoftAP client MAC, formatted with hyphens, or `nobody`

`WifiManager` now tracks the current SoftAP client via `esp_wifi_ap_get_sta_list()` and increments the count whenever a client appears after no client, or a different client appears. The tracking resets when Wi-Fi/AP starts or stops.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` succeeded. The first build run had a transient missing `version.c.o` artifact, rerunning regenerated it and linked cleanly.

commit_message: cosmetic changes to the AP view

user_prompt: does the change you just made effect how the ESPAsyncWebServer and AsyncTCP works?
agent_reply: Yes, but probably not the way youâ€™re most worried about.

`ESPAsyncWebServer` / `AsyncTCP` should still work at the TCP socket level because the new path still creates the ESP netif through `wifiLowLevelInit(false)`, applies `esp_wifi_set_mode(WIFI_MODE_AP)`, configures `WIFI_IF_AP`, then calls `esp_wifi_start()`. AsyncTCP listens through lwIP, not through `WiFi.softAP()` specifically.

The risky part is Arduinoâ€™s AP wrapper bookkeeping. Because we now bypass `WiFi.softAP()` / `WiFi.AP.begin()`, helpers like `WiFi.softAPIP()` may return `0.0.0.0` or stale data even though the AP itself is up. I already dodged that in the new log line, but I found existing uses here that may need the same treatment:

So: the web server should still accept connections by IP, but the UI/API may report the wrong AP IP unless we move those call sites to a lower-level AP IP helper too. Also, mDNS is the one â€śkeep an eye on itâ€ť item, since it can be sensitive to interface/event setup.
Yes, but probably not the way youâ€™re most worried about.

`ESPAsyncWebServer` / `AsyncTCP` should still work at the TCP socket level because the new path still creates the ESP netif through `wifiLowLevelInit(false)`, applies `esp_wifi_set_mode(WIFI_MODE_AP)`, configures `WIFI_IF_AP`, then calls `esp_wifi_start()`. AsyncTCP listens through lwIP, not through `WiFi.softAP()` specifically.

The risky part is Arduinoâ€™s AP wrapper bookkeeping. Because we now bypass `WiFi.softAP()` / `WiFi.AP.begin()`, helpers like `WiFi.softAPIP()` may return `0.0.0.0` or stale data even though the AP itself is up. I already dodged that in the new log line, but I found existing uses here that may need the same treatment:

So: the web server should still accept connections by IP, but the UI/API may report the wrong AP IP unless we move those call sites to a lower-level AP IP helper too. Also, mDNS is the one â€śkeep an eye on itâ€ť item, since it can be sensitive to interface/event setup.

user_prompt: ### Task: QR Code for Wi-Fi AP
Create a `QrCodeView.cpp` inside `lib\FlyGuiViews`, and the associated header file.
The configuration of this view simply takes a string, it remembers the string.

Upon loading, it makes a huge white filled rectangle, starting 1 pixel below the top bar, filling up the rest of the screen. Then it draws a QR code with the string, as big as possible centered within this filled rectangle.
I believe Iâ€™ve seen example code, itâ€™s the function `drawQRCode` of the display object.
The view is dismissed on any touch, as if it was a `ModalDialog` , going back to `WifiApModeView`, which is the only place this will be used

Inside `WifiApModeView`, if the user long-holds the eye icon, no matter what state, for 5 seconds, draw a series of spaced white lines just like how we fade out the screen with black lines first, upon release of touch (even if off target), show the `QrCodeView` configured for instant Wi-Fi configuration if a smartphone sees the QR code
agent_reply: Implemented the QR Wi-Fi AP flow.

Added `QrCodeView` under `lib/FlyGuiViews`, registered it in `FlyGui`, and wired `WifiApModeView` so a 5-second hold on the eye icon draws the white-line fade, then opens the QR view on release even if the touch is off-target. Short taps on the eye still toggle the hidden credentials as before.

The QR payload is standard smartphone Wi-Fi config format: `WIFI:T:WPA;S:...;P:...;H:false;;`, with escaping for special characters. The QR view uses the displayâ€™s `qrcode(...)` API, fills the area below the top bar white, centers the largest square QR code possible, and dismisses back to AP mode on any touch/button press.
Only warning was the existing ESP-FTP-Server deprecated `NetworkServer::available()` warning.

commit_message: fixed a lot of web issues

user_prompt: const IPAddress    ip   = is_ap ? WiFi.softAPIP() : WiFi.localIP();
will the highlighted line work? we have discovered many instances that it might not because we start the soft AP with lower level calls
agent_reply: No, I would not trust that line for AP mode. Since your AP path is configured through lower-level `esp_wifi` / `esp_netif`, `WiFi.softAPIP()` can be stale or wrong depending on what Arduinoâ€™s WiFi wrapper thinks happened.
I changed it so production code uses a new manager-owned helper:
- It uses the existing `current_soft_ap_ip()` path, which reads `WIFI_AP_DEF` through `esp_netif_get_ip_info`
- Updated the AP credential screen and web server self-IP path too
`WiFi.softAPIP()` now only remains in tests that start AP through Arduino directly.

user_prompt: rename these highlighted getters to `get_view_xyz();`, the word `all_init_` isn't useful and adds confusion about their purpose
and get rid of the functions that wrap them
agent_reply: Renamed the view accessors from `all_init_*_view()` to `get_view_*()` across the project, including the extra same-pattern ones like main screen, cloud upload, and QR code.
Updated callers in `main.cpp`, [src/main_callbacks.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main_callbacks.cpp), and the few library files that referenced the wrappers.

user_prompt: also make sure every `.cpp` file inside `lib\FlyGuiViews` meet the guidelines at `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md`, however, it is also important that their code layout look almost the same between them as they do kind of inherit from the same base classes sometimes
agent_reply: The common shape is now: constants/types/prototypes first, primary view/API methods next, supporting/drawing/formatting helpers later. I also kept the FlyGui view files visually aligned with each other by using the same lightweight section markers and putting helper blocks at the bottom.
- `src\version.c` build churn was restored; final status only shows the intended layout files.
